description: CT Chest / Abdomen / Pelvis with IV contrast
indication: Severe unintentional weight loss (30%), dysphagia, multi-system illness; rule out malignancy and other anatomic process
technique: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed with IV contrast. 5 mm axial reconstructions with coronal and sagittal reformats.
findings:
CHEST:
- Lung parenchyma: No focal consolidation, mass, or nodule. Lung volumes reduced. No interstitial lung disease pattern. No air trapping. No ground glass opacity to suggest organizing pneumonia.
- Pleura: Small bilateral pleural effusions (right ~150 mL, left ~120 mL). Smooth pleural surface; no nodularity to suggest malignancy.
- Mediastinum: No lymphadenopathy. No mediastinal mass. Trace pericardial effusion present (estimated <5 mm). Heart size normal accounting for AP CXR finding.
- Esophagus: Distal esophagus distended with retained secretions/fluid. Wall thickness normal. No mass.
- Thymus: Atrophic appearance consistent with age and disease.
- Bones: Diffuse osteopenia of the ribs, vertebrae, and proximal humeri.
- Muscles: GENERALIZED, SEVERE MUSCLE WASTING. Pectoralis, deltoid, scapular, and paraspinal musculature markedly reduced in bulk. No discrete masses or focal abscesses in muscle.
- Soft tissue: Anasarca of chest wall.

ABDOMEN:
- Liver: Normal size. Smooth contour. No focal lesions. No biliary dilation. No ascites within Morison's pouch on CT (small-volume ascites elsewhere — see below).
- Spleen: Normal size (10.6 cm). No focal lesions.
- Pancreas: Atrophic appearance with prominent main pancreatic duct (subjective; not pathologic). No mass.
- Adrenals: Bilaterally normal.
- Kidneys: Bilateral kidneys normal in size and contour. No hydronephrosis. No nephrolithiasis. Mild perinephric edema.
- Bowel: No wall thickening. No mass. No obstruction. No definite enteritis or colitis. Loops contain modest air; otherwise unremarkable.
- Lymph nodes: No abdominal or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.
- Ascites: Small-volume free fluid in the pelvis and around the bowel loops, consistent with low-albumin third-spacing.
- Bones: Osteopenia. No lytic or blastic lesions.
- Muscles: SEVERE WASTING of the paraspinal, psoas, iliopsoas, and abdominal wall musculature. Iliopsoas dimensions markedly below expected for age and sex.
- Soft tissue: Generalized subcutaneous edema.

PELVIS:
- Bladder: Normal. No mass.
- Uterus and adnexa: Normal size; no mass; small free fluid in cul-de-sac (ascites).
- Bones: Osteopenia.
- Muscles: SEVERE WASTING of gluteal and pelvic floor musculature. Gluteus maximus, medius, and minimus all atrophic.
- Soft tissues: Subcutaneous edema; lower extremity edema where included on CT.

INCIDENTAL: No bone marrow signal abnormality on routine windows. No focal infectious collection. No abscess.
impression:
1. SEVERE GENERALIZED MUSCLE WASTING involving the chest wall, paraspinal, psoas, iliopsoas, gluteal, and pelvic muscles. Findings consistent with profound cachexia and / or longstanding myopathy.
2. SMALL BILATERAL PLEURAL EFFUSIONS and SMALL VOLUME ASCITES — consistent with hypoalbuminemic third-spacing in the setting of severe protein-energy malnutrition.
3. TRACE PERICARDIAL EFFUSION — recommend echocardiogram for further characterization and to assess myocardial function in this patient with possible cardiac involvement of SLE / myositis.
4. ANASARCA / GENERALIZED SUBCUTANEOUS EDEMA.
5. DIFFUSE OSTEOPENIA — consistent with chronic illness, steroid exposure, and nutritional insufficiency.
6. NO EVIDENCE OF MALIGNANCY: No mass, no abnormal lymphadenopathy, no specific organ lesion to suggest occult neoplasm.
7. NO LYMPHADENOPATHY to suggest lymphoma or active infection.
8. NO HEPATOSPLENOMEGALY. Liver, spleen, kidneys, pancreas appear structurally normal.
9. NO ANATOMIC SOURCE FOR DYSPHAGIA on CT (no esophageal mass, no extrinsic compression, no focal wall thickening). Findings consistent with a NEUROMUSCULAR rather than anatomic etiology for the dysphagia.

These findings, taken together with the clinical presentation and laboratory abnormalities, support a unifying diagnosis of severe autoimmune myositis with associated dysphagia leading to (or worsening) profound protein-calorie malnutrition. No alternative anatomic etiology (malignancy, infection, structural disease) identified by this study.
comparison: No prior CT available for comparison.
